title: Bloom: Sacred Spring Cacao Ceremony, Sound Bath Meditation, Nature & Art
address: 900 N Birch Rd, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33304
shortWhy: Bloom live at Bonnet House Museum & Gardens.
editorialWhy: Register Here! Step into a rejuvenating morning to celebrate the changing season with nourishment for your body, mind, and spirit. Set against the backdrop of the lush Bonnet House Museum and Gardens on Fort Lauderdale Beach, this multi-sensory, holistic experience invites you to open your heart, explore, connect, and elevate your spirit. And also enjoy the ZimSculpt exhibit & More Featuring special guests Erik Guru Lieux and Lisa Pumper, our resident teacher. ‍♀️ DETAILS: Main Event: 9:00 AM – 11:00 AM (Gate opens for check-in at 8:30 AM) Cacao Meditation Ceremony: A sacred cacao ceremony and elixir to open your heart and set the tone for the day. ️ Somatic Release & Breathwork: Ground your energy with a few gentle movements and breathwork (suitable for everyone). Sound Healing Journey: Experience deep relaxation and meditative stillness with a live sound bath featuring soothing instruments and vibrational healing such as gongs, flutes, crystal sound bowls, and other healing tools. Silent Walk and Rejuvenating Forest Bath with Live Healing Flute. Post Event: (11:15 AM onwards) After the main festivities, take time to immerse yourself in the beautiful surroundings. ZimSculpt Exhibit & Marketplace: Enjoy a unique outdoor art experience in harmony with nature, also featuring a vibrant market with art crafted from stone, crystal, and other natural materials. Visit the Bonnet House Gift Shop & Café for unique finds and light refreshments. ☕️ With Upgraded Tickets Purchased at the Museum Shop: Museum Home Tour: Get a glimpse into the creative space rich in history and inspiration. Immerse yourself fully with a self-guided tour of the house and gardens, explore rotating exhibits in the Gallery, and listen to delightful stories from staff and volunteers. Walk through nature trails and under canopies for more rejuvenating Forest Bathing; take a moment by the water or sit under a tree to journal or read. ✍️ DATE: Friday. March 20 – first day of spring! ⏰TIME: 9:00 am – 11:00 am ( check in 8:30 am ) ALL TICKETS INCLUDE: ✅ Cacao Ceremony Meditation ✅ Multi-layered Sound Healing Experience ✅ ZIM Sculpture Exhibit & Market ✅ Forest Bath Walk & Flute ✅ Free Private Parking Tickets: $ 50 Member, $ 55 Future Member Note: The Café/Gift Shop opens at 11:00 AM. *Allow extra time for travel and parking. Class takes place before Bonnet House opens to the public. If the main gate is closed, kindly ring the bell. HOW TO PREPARE: DRESS: Wear comfortable attire for sitting/lying down and suitable shoes for walking in nature. BRING: A bag for your items, a water bottle, a beach towel, a blanket, or a yoga mat for sitting or lying on the veranda, under trees, or elsewhere after the event. Consider bringing a journal or a book if you plan to stay into the afternoon. ✨ Chairs will be available for those who prefer sitting during the Cacao ceremony. Yoga mat rentals will be available for $5 cash each for travelers. ENTRANCE: Opens at 8:30 AM. The main entrance with private parking is located on the north/west side of the Bonnet House, accessible from Sunrise Blvd, on the southeast side of the bridge. Enter through the west gate on NE 9th Street. (Please allow extra travel time if the bridge is raised, for parking, and check-in. The event will start promptly at 9:00 AM.) ABOUT: Erik “GURU” Lieux: Urban Alchemist, DJ, Ceremonial Cacao Meditation and Movement Guide, Artist, Holistic and Integrative Counselor, and Founder and Creator of South Florida’s artisan superfood/super herb elixir and powder line – Shamanic Tonic http://shamanictonic.com | http://integratewitherik.com | http://nowitsforever.com | IG: @ShamanicTonic Lisa Pumper: Resident Yoga and Meditation Teacher at Bonnet House, Community Activator, Producer & Founder of Yoga cOMmUNITY of Florida Events & Ft Lauderdale Beach Yoga and Fit, E-RYT 500, YACEP since 2004 http://beachyogafit.com | IG: @southfloridayogi EVENT QUESTIONS & RESERVE A MAT RENTAL: Email: info@YogacommunityFL.com SPECIAL EVENT REFUND INFO: All sales are final. No refunds or credits will be issued. We are not responsible for any personal reasons preventing you from attending special events, such as travel, health issues, or other. As mentioned above, please ensure you read all the information to ensure you have all the details. Register Here! Outdoors at Bonnet House Museum & Gardens in Fort Lauderdale Beach. Free to attend.

title: Yoga on the Veranda
address: 900 N Birch Rd, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33304
shortWhy: Free outdoor wellness in Fort Lauderdale Beach.
editorialWhy: YOGA & SOUND BATH Register Here! Slow Vinyasa Flow. Sound Bath. Tropical Tranquility Step into a tropical oasis, enveloped by the nurturing embrace of nature’s beauty, as you flow gently through a Yoga experience for all, harmonized with a symphony of global melodies. In this serene journey, yoga postures gracefully intertwine through a sequence of movements, enhancing your flexibility and mobility while centering your breath in harmony with motion. This sacred practice cultivates stability, strength, coordination, balance, observation, and awareness, inviting a profound connection with the natural world. The experience culminates in a guided relaxation, an aromatherapy refresher, and a soothing sound bath meditation with a healing instrument, leaving your spirit renewed and uplifted. LEAD TEACHER: Lisa Pumper, E-RYT 500, YACEP Resident BH Yoga teacher & SoFlo leading teacher, producer, and founder of Fort Lauderdale Beach Yoga powered by Yoga cOMmUNITY of Florida Lisa will demonstrate poses and techniques while offering clear instructions, including modifications that cater to all skill levels, ensuring everyone can fully enjoy the experience. HOW TO PREPARE: TIME: Class starts at 9:00 am – check-in opens at 8:30 am. Duration 90 minutes Give extra time for travel, parking, and walking up to the museum. BYO: Yoga mat, any props, a large towel, a bottle of water, and a bag to carry your items. (*travelers can rent a yoga mat for $5 cash) The Gift Shop Cafe opens at 11:00 am for additional needs. PARTICIPATION WAIVER: https://form.jotform.com/yogacommunityfl/bh-yoga LOCATION: *Classes take place regardless of the weather, whether it’s sunny or rainy. They are conducted on the paved surfaces and in the covered areas of either the Veranda or the Courtyard at the Bonnet Museum home, both of which provide breathtaking views of nature. TICKET OPTIONS: $ 25 Members / $30 Non-Members: Includes Yoga class, cool-down aromatherapy refresher, inspirational note card, extra free time after class, and free private parking. $45 Non-Members: Includes yoga class and a self-guided house and grounds tour. Spend the morning in tropical tranquility after class with a self-guided tour of the house and gardens, see the rotating exhibits in the Gallery, listen to sweet tour stories from staff and volunteers, sit by the garden fountain, the pond, or lake, walk through forest trails, and breathe in the peaceful views. Space is very limited. Pre-registration is required to secure your spot. Class is rain or shine. Tickets are non-refundable. CLASS QUESTIONS: Contact Lisa @ info@yogacommunityfl.com All sales are final. No refunds or exchanges. Register Here! Outdoors at Bonnet House Museum & Gardens in Fort Lauderdale Beach. Free to attend.

title: Speaker Series – March 9
address: 900 N Birch Rd, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33304
shortWhy: Culture at Bonnet House Museum & Gardens, Fort Lauderdale Beach.
editorialWhy: History, Art, and Culture: A Contemporary Look featuring Emmanuel George. Emmanuel George is a Dominican-American archivist, filmmaker & arts organizer based in Hollywood, Florida. Emmanuel’s love of history began in childhood when his father Emanuel George, Sr. would teach him Black history in efforts to highlight what he thought Emmanuel would not be taught in public school. Emmanuel credits his father’s teachings to his ability to seek out overlooked narratives. In 2015, after taking a leave of absence from pursuing his bachelor’s degree in film from Full Sail University, Emmanuel found himself joining a group of local activists in Miami-Dade County who were advocating on behalf of its residents to fight displacement. During this time, Emmanuel spoke with local residents and learned about the history of Black Miami. This curiosity led Emmanuel to begin submerging himself in learning the Black history of his home community, Broward County. In 2016, Emmanuel George produced his first film under the Black Broward Film Project, entitled A Tale of Sibling Communities; Danie and Liberia a documentary about the shared relationship between two Black communities in Broward County. The film featured oral interviews of local pioneers and historical landmarks that are staples to the community. Emmanuel followed this up with his 2021 film entitled Stories from our Ancestors: An Ode to Attucks High School ; a film focusing on how the closing of a historically Black High school impacted the community of Liberia and its neighboring communities: Danie, Carver Ranches, Hallandale, Ojus, and Davie. In 2021, under the mentorship of esteemed historian Kitty Oliver, Emmanuel landed a fellowship with Lake Wales Museum where he interviewed and archived stories of residents from the historic Lincoln Avenue community. From there, Emmanuel would complete a fellowship with Cogenerators (formerly Encore ) and work with Florida International University’s Wolfson Public Humanities Lab , the Delray Beach Historical Society , the African American Research Library and the Old Dillard Museum , further expanding the ways he shares and preserves local Black history. Most recently, Emmanuel completed a fellowship with Temple University , serving as the Community Archivist for the Association for the Study of African American Life and History (ASALH). In-person or via Zoom. Limited seating. Pre-registration required Free for Members, $5 for Future Members (All sales are final and tickets are non-refundable). Register Here In Person Register Here ZOOM An email will be sent to all Zoom registrants with the link and instructions prior to the lecture. At Bonnet House Museum & Gardens in Fort Lauderdale Beach. Free to attend.

title: Oh HONEY
address: 900 N Birch Rd, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33304
shortWhy: Art at Bonnet House Museum & Gardens, Fort Lauderdale Beach.
editorialWhy: “OH HONEY in your bonnet” at Bonnet House with Joanna Diamond Date: Saturday, April 25, 2026 Time: 10 am – 12:30 pm This exciting class dives into the incredible, edible world of honey — that precious natural food that sustains honey bees and delights us in countless ways. You’ll learn how honey and honeycomb are made, how honey travels from hive to jar, its many health benefits and uses, what causes honey to crystallize, and why “organic honey” is often misunderstood. We’ll also explore the massive issue of global honey fraud and how to identify the real thing. We’ll compare wildflower (poly‑varietal) honey with mono‑varietal honeys such as orange blossom or clover, and discuss which varieties may offer greater health benefits. You’ll also get the truth about manuka honey — and whether the price tag is justified. Discover just how hard honey bees work to produce honey (did you know one worker bee makes only one‑twelfth of a teaspoon in her entire lifetime?). You’ll also learn about the process of honey production, the meaning of honey color, and the difference between raw and processed supermarket honey. The class includes a fun and surprising honey‑tasting session , where you’ll try a variety of samples and guess whether each one is raw honey straight from the hive or processed honey from a store. You’ll taste amazing creamed honey, whipped honey, foamed honey, ultra‑waxy honey, and some of the purest raw honey in the world. Please Note: Raw wildflower honey will be available for purchase after class, along with Orchid Bee Feeders and Honey Bee Revival Kits — perfect for helping those tired little bees you may find on the ground. About Instructor Joanna Diamond Born and raised in South Africa, Joanna is passionate about saving honey bees and educating the public on their extraordinary importance. She spent four and a half years volunteering with a Bee Rescue & Removal charity, has assisted with countless rescues, is a registered beekeeper, and has earned the title of Master Beekeeper after three years of intensive study with the University of Florida/IFAS. She works closely with a South Florida beekeeper and live bee removal specialist and strongly advocates for humane removal and protection of honey bees. $30 Members • $40 Future Members Outdoors at Bonnet House Museum & Gardens in Fort Lauderdale Beach. Free to attend.
description: “OH HONEY in your bonnet” at Bonnet House with Joanna Diamond Date: Saturday, April 25, 2026 Time: 10 am – 12:30 pm This exciting class dives into the incredible, edible world of honey — that precious natural food that sustains honey bees and delights us in countless ways. You’ll learn how honey and honeycomb are made, how honey travels from hive to jar, its many health benefits and uses, what causes honey to crystallize, and why “organic honey” is often misunderstood. We’ll also explore the massive issue of global honey fraud and how to identify the real thing. We’ll compare wildflower (poly‑varietal) honey with mono‑varietal honeys such as orange blossom or clover, and discuss which varieties may offer greater health benefits. You’ll also get the truth about manuka honey — and whether the price tag is justified. Discover just how hard honey bees work to produce honey (did you know one worker bee makes only one‑twelfth of a teaspoon in her entire lifetime?). You’ll also learn about the process of honey production, the meaning of honey color, and the difference between raw and processed supermarket honey. The class includes a fun and surprising honey‑tasting session , where you’ll try a variety of samples and guess whether each one is raw honey straight from the hive or processed honey from a store. You’ll taste amazing creamed honey, whipped honey, foamed honey, ultra‑waxy honey, and some of the purest raw honey in the world. Please Note: Raw wildflower honey will be available for purchase after class, along with Orchid Bee Feeders and Honey Bee Revival Kits — perfect for helping those tired little bees you may find on the ground. About Instructor Joanna Diamond Born and raised in South Africa, Joanna is passionate about saving honey bees and educating the public on their extraordinary importance. She spent four and a half years volunteering with a Bee Rescue & Removal charity, has assisted with countless rescues, is a registered beekeeper, and has earned the title of Master Beekeeper after three years of intensive study with the University of Florida/IFAS. She works closely with a South Florida beekeeper and live bee removal specialist and strongly advocates for humane removal and protection of honey bees. $30 Members • $40 Future Members

address: 900 N Birch Rd, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33304

editorialWhy: Learning in Bloom: An FAU and Bonnet House Collaboration 3-Part Series Dates: Wednesday, January 21st Monday, February 23rd Monday, March 30th Schedule: 1:00-2:00 PM Lecture (45min) and Q & A (15min) Title: The Future of Florida’s Coasts: What We Can Learn from the Past Speaker: Evan Bennett, PhD, Professor, Director, Voting Rights History Civic Literacy Project Dept. of History Summary: Florida’s coastal spaces face challenges, from development to pollution, that are set to grow more critical in the next century. Dr. Evan Bennett argues the challenges are best understood as historical problems and should be viewed from a historical perspective. Dr. Bennett will take you on a journey through 2,000 years of Florida coastal history and show how people have interacted with the shoreline through time. He’ll also help you understand the big challenges we’re facing today, like development, pollution, and sea level rise, all through the lens of the past. Limited seating is available. Pre-registration is required. $5 for Members, $10 for Non-members. (All sales are final, and tickets are non-refundable.)
